q:
This warning sign indicates that there is a pedestrian crossing further ahead.
May this pedestrian cross the roadway here?

A. Yes.
B. No.
e:
LESSON 7
If there is a pedestrian crossing within 20 metres, pedestrians must use it.
The warning sign indicates that a pedestrian crossing is located about 150 metres ahead.
➡️ The pedestrian may cross here.
clean_q:
This warning sign indicates that there is a pedestrian crossing further ahead.
May this pedestrian cross the roadway here?
A. Yes.
B. No.
clean_e:
LESSON 7
If there is a pedestrian crossing within 20 metres, pedestrians must use it.
The warning sign indicates that a pedestrian crossing is located about 150 metres ahead.
➡️ The pedestrian may cross here.

q:
May the car stop at that place within a built-up area to let a passenger get in?

A. Yes.
B. No.
e:
LESSON 7
It is forbidden to park or stop on a pedestrian crossing, even to let someone get in or out.
It is also forbidden to park or to stop on the roadway within 5 metres before the crossing (parking on the verge is allowed if possible).
The same rule applies to crossings for cyclists and two-wheeled mopeds.
After the crossing, parking and stopping are allowed, unless prohibited by traffic signs.
clean_q:
May the car stop at that place within a built-up area to let a passenger get in?
A. Yes.
B. No.
clean_e:
LESSON 7
It is forbidden to park or stop on a pedestrian crossing, even to let someone get in or out.
It is also forbidden to park or to stop on the roadway within 5 metres before the crossing (parking on the verge is allowed if possible).
The same rule applies to crossings for cyclists and two-wheeled mopeds.
After the crossing, parking and stopping are allowed, unless prohibited by traffic signs.

q:
A girl is pushing her moped on the road. According to the traffic regulations she is …

A. ... a driver.
B. ... a pedestrian.
e:
LESSON 7 – ‘The pedestrians’.
Someone who pushes a moped is for the traffic regulations code a pedestrian.
Someone who pushes a car or motorcycle is for the traffic regulations code a driver.
clean_q:
A girl is pushing her moped on the road. According to the traffic regulations she is … 
A. ... a driver.
B. ... a pedestrian.
clean_e:
LESSON 7 – ‘The pedestrians’.
Someone who pushes a moped is for the traffic regulations code a pedestrian.
Someone who pushes a car or motorcycle is for the traffic regulations code a driver.

q:
Which mopeds are allowed to ride past this sign?

A. Class A.
B. Class B.
C. No mopeds.
e:
LESSON 7 – ‘The pedestrians’.
Pedestrian and bicycle under each other: no mopeds are allowed.
Pedestrian and bicycle next to each other: only class A mopeds are allowed.
clean_q:
Which mopeds are allowed to ride past this sign? 
A. Class A.
B. Class B.
C. No mopeds.
clean_e:
LESSON 7 – ‘The pedestrians’.
Pedestrian and bicycle under each other: no mopeds are allowed.
Pedestrian and bicycle next to each other: only class A mopeds are allowed.

q:
A pedestrian is not using or going to use the pedestrian crossing.
Is the brown car allowed to overtake the red one on the left?
e:
LESSON 7 – ‘The pedestrians’.
You may not overtake a driver on the left who slows down or stops in front of a zebra crossing.
clean_q:
A pedestrian is not using or going to use the pedestrian crossing. 
Is the brown car allowed to overtake the red one on the  left?
clean_e:
LESSON 7 – ‘The pedestrians’.
You may not overtake a driver on the left who slows down or stops in front of a zebra crossing.

q:
A winged zebra crossing.
Who is allowed to cross here?

A. Only pedestrians.
B. Pedestrians and cyclists.
clean_q:
A winged zebra crossing.
Who is allowed to cross here?
A. Only pedestrians.
B. Pedestrians and cyclists.

q:
The man walking next to the horse is …

A. ... a pedestrian.
B. ... a driver.
e:
LESSON 7 – ‘The pedestrians’.
A horseback rider or a person accompanying a horse is not a pedestrian but a driver.
clean_q:
The man walking next to the horse is … 
A. ... a pedestrian.
B. ... a driver.
clean_e:
LESSON 7 – ‘The pedestrians’.
A horseback rider or a person accompanying a horse is not a pedestrian but a driver.

q:
A school bus stops outside a built-up area, not at a bus stop but on the roadway near a house. The driver switches on the four hazard lights.
How do you react?

A. You just continue driving, because it is not a bus stop.
B. You slow down sharply and stop if necessary because children may get off.
e:
LESSON 7
School transport vehicles are recognizable by a yellow sign (at least 0.40 m), clearly visible at the front and rear of the vehicle. This sign must be removed or covered when the vehicle is not being used for school transport.
Be extra cautious and reduce your speed.
Stop if necessary when you see a school transport vehicle switching on its indicators or hazard lights, which means children are about to get on or off. This applies both to vehicles that are stationary and to those that stop on the roadway.
clean_q:
A school bus stops outside a built-up area, not at a bus stop but on the roadway near a house. The driver switches on the four hazard lights.
How do you react?
A. You just continue driving, because it is not a bus stop.
B. You slow down sharply and stop if necessary because children may get off.
clean_e:
LESSON 7
School transport vehicles are recognizable by a yellow sign (at least 0.40 m), clearly visible at the front and rear of the vehicle. This sign must be removed or covered when the vehicle is not being used for school transport.
Be extra cautious and reduce your speed.
Stop if necessary when you see a school transport vehicle switching on its indicators or hazard lights, which means children are about to get on or off. This applies both to vehicles that are stationary and to those that stop on the roadway.

q:
Someone pushing a moped is …

A. ... a pedestrian.
B. ... a driver.
e:
LESSON 7 – ‘The pedestrians’.
Who pushes a moped is a pedestrian.
He should step on the footpath.
Who pushes a motorcycle or car, is a driver.
clean_q:
Someone pushing a moped is … 
A. ... a pedestrian.
B. ... a driver.
clean_e:
LESSON 7 – ‘The pedestrians’.
Who pushes a moped is a pedestrian.
He should step on the footpath.
Who pushes a motorcycle or car, is a driver.

q: You are not allowed to be stationary or to park within (…) meters in front of the pedestrian crossing.
e:
LESSON 7 – ‘The pedestrians’.
You are not allowed to be stationary or to park on a zebra crossing, or on the road within 5 meters of a zebra crossing.
Past a zebra crossing you are allowed to park and to be stationary (except when traffic signs prohibit it).
clean_q: You are not allowed to be stationary or to park within (…) meters in front of the pedestrian crossing.
clean_e:
LESSON 7 – ‘The pedestrians’.
You are not allowed to be stationary or to park on a zebra crossing, or on the road within 5 meters of a zebra crossing.
Past a zebra crossing you are allowed to park and to be stationary (except when traffic signs prohibit it).

q:
Are you allowed to drive your car into this street?

A. Only if your parents, brother or sister live there.
B. If family lives there.
C. No.
e: You need to have a special permission.
clean_q:
Are you allowed to drive your car into this street?

q:
The pedestrian is about to cross over the road.

A. The car must give priority.
B. The pedestrian must give priority.
e:
LESSON 7 – ‘The pedestrians’.
Pedestrians on the zebra crossing or intending to use it, must get priority.
A pedestrian who wants to cross the road where there is no zebra crossing, he himself has to give priority.

LESSON 7 – ‘The pedestrians’.

A pedestrian who wants to cross the road where there is no zebra crossing, he himself has to give priority.

Pedestrians on the zebra crossing or intending to use it, must get priority.
A pedestrian who wants to cross the road where there is no zebra crossing, he himself has to give priority.

q:
A cyclist steps next to his bike. He wants to cross the road.
Where does he have priority?

A. At sign A.
B. At sign B.
C. At sign A and B.
clean_q:
A cyclist steps next to his bike. He wants to cross the road.

B. At sign B.
C. At sign A and B.

q:
A dog walking in the street is according to the traffic regulations …

A. ... a pedestrian.
B. ... a road user.
C. ... no pedestrian nor road user.
e:
LESSON 7 – ‘The pedestrians’.
According to the traffic regulations a road user is anyone who uses the public road. So they are persons.
An animal like a horse or a dog and things like a bicycle or a car are not road users.
clean_q:
A dog walking in the street is according to the traffic regulations … 
A. ... a pedestrian.
B. ... a road user.
C. ... no pedestrian nor road user.
clean_e:
LESSON 7 – ‘The pedestrians’.
According to the traffic regulations a road user is anyone who uses the public road. So they are persons.
An animal like a horse or a dog and things like a bicycle or a car are not road users.

q:
An adult wants to cross the road with young children.
How should you react?

A. You continue driving because there is no pedestrian crossing.
B. You stop and let the children cross calmly.
C. You pay extra attention and are ready to brake if a child suddenly steps onto the road.
e:
DEFENSIVE DRIVING
Children can behave unpredictably and may suddenly step onto the road.
You adjust your speed, keep the situation under control, and are ready to brake immediately.

clean_e:
DEFENSIVE DRIVING
Children can behave unpredictably and may suddenly step onto the road.
You adjust your speed, keep the situation under control, and are ready to brake immediately.

q:
What does this sign mean?

A. No entry to pedestrians.
B. Pedestrians can cross over the road.
C. No entry to all road users, except pedestrians.
e:
LESSON 7 – ‘The pedestrians’.
Meaning: no entry for pedestrians.
Prohibitive sign.

q:
A car may not park on the roadway within 5 metres before a pedestrian or cyclist crossing.
But may a driver stop there to let someone get in?

A. Yes.
B. No.
e:
LESSON 7
It is forbidden to park or stop on a pedestrian crossing, even to let someone get in or out.
It is also forbidden to park or to stop on the roadway within 5 metres before the crossing (parking on the verge is allowed if possible).
The same rule applies to crossings for cyclists and two-wheeled mopeds.
After the crossing, parking and stopping are allowed, unless prohibited by traffic signs.

q:
The indication or information sign is placed at the pedestrian crossing.
Which sign is the indication sign?

A. The red sign.
B. The blue sign.
e:
LESSON 7 – ‘The pedestrians’.
The red sign is a warning or danger sign and is placed 150 meters in front of the crossover.

A. The red sign. 
B. The blue sign.
clean_e:
LESSON 7 – ‘The pedestrians’.
The red sign is a warning or danger sign and is placed 150 meters in front of the crossover.

q:
Traffic is moving very slowly and keeps stopping.
If traffic comes to a halt again, are you allowed to stop on a pedestrian crossing?

A. Yes.
B. No, you must stop before the pedestrian crossing.
e:
You are not allowed to stop or park on a pedestrian crossing.
This rule is strict.
Even in a traffic jam, you may not stop, not even briefly, on a pedestrian crossing to follow traffic.
Note: in a traffic jam, stopping on an elevated section is allowed.

A. Yes.
B. No, you must stop before the pedestrian crossing.
clean_e:
You are not allowed to stop or park on a pedestrian crossing.
This rule is strict.
Even in a traffic jam, you may not stop, not even briefly, on a pedestrian crossing to follow traffic.
Note: in a traffic jam, stopping on an elevated section is allowed.

q: If there is a pedestrian crossing or a zebra crossing within (…) meters, pedestrians must use it.
e:
LESSON 7 – ‘The pedestrians’.
A pedestrian who wants to cross over the road where there is no zebra crossing, he himself has to give priority.The message is: always be careful.
(Before 31/5/2019 it was 30 meters).

LESSON 7 – ‘The pedestrians’.

(Before 31/5/2019 it was 30 meters).

q: In a Zone 30, drivers must always give way to pedestrians, even if there is no zebra crossing.
clean_q: In a Zone 30, drivers must always give way to pedestrians, even if there is no zebra crossing.

q:
Beyond these signs, a child wants to cross the street in a place where there is no pedestrian crossing.
Do you need to stop and give priority to the child?
e:
LESSON 7 – ‘The pedestrians’.
A pedestrian who wants to cross the roadway in a place where there is no pedestrian crossing should itself give priority. However, the message is that you should always be careful.

q:
Your girlfriend lives in this pedestrian zone.
Can you drive your car to her home?
e:
LESSON 7
It is forbidden to drive a car in a Belgian pedestrian zone (sign F103).
Access is only allowed with a special permit, usually for residents or for loading and unloading during specific hours.
If you enter without permission, you risk a fine.
Drivers who are allowed to enter must:
• drive at walking pace
• always give way to pedestrians
• stop if necessary
• never endanger or hinder pedestrians.

• always give way to pedestrians
• stop if necessary
• never endanger or hinder pedestrians.

q:
The maximum permitted speed in this built-up area is 30 km/h.
Must you give way if pedestrians cross the road?

A. Yes, because the speed limit is 30 km/h.
B. No, they only have priority on a pedestrian crossing.
e:
LESSON 7
A pedestrian who wants to cross the road at a place where there is no pedestrian crossing must give way themselves.
Nevertheless, drivers must always be cautious.
